title: Staking Crypto - Open Lido App
meta_description: staking crypto with Lido: open the official app, connect a wallet, stake ETH, and receive stETH for Ethereum liquid staking.
hero_h2: Put ETH to work through liquid staking
hero_lead: staking crypto with Lido routes ETH holders to the official liquid staking app to connect a wallet, deposit ETH, and receive stETH while keeping DeFi usability.
what_is: Lido is an Ethereum-focused liquid staking protocol that lets users stake ETH and mint stETH, a transferable token representing staked ETH. See Lido Docs for protocol mechanics and risk disclosures.

how.d: Go to stake.lido.fi and verify the domain before connecting a wallet.
Use a self-custody Ethereum wallet and review every prompt in the wallet interface.
Enter an ETH amount, confirm the transaction, and receive stETH after it settles on-chain.
Monitor the position in the app; withdrawals and secondary-market routes depend on network conditions, gas, and liquidity.

points.p: stETH is designed as a transferable utility token for staked ETH, so it can be held or used in compatible DeFi apps.
Ethereum staking helps secure the network; compare pooled staking with solo staking on ethereum.org before choosing a route.
Validator performance, smart contracts, governance, gas costs, and stETH market pricing can affect outcomes; review Lido’s official documentation first.

faq.q: How does staking crypto through Lido work?
faq.a: You deposit ETH through Lido’s app and receive stETH, which represents staked ETH under Lido protocol mechanics. Read the official docs before depositing.

faq.q: Is staking crypto with Lido the same as running a validator?
faq.a: No. Running your own Ethereum validator requires 32 ETH and node operations; Lido is a third-party pooled liquid staking protocol with different trust and smart contract risks.

faq.q: What do I receive when staking crypto on the app?
faq.a: After the ETH deposit confirms, the app mints stETH on Ethereum, which is transferable and usable where supported by wallets and DeFi protocols.

faq.q: Can I withdraw after staking crypto?
faq.a: Lido provides a withdrawals flow, and stETH may also trade on secondary markets; timing, exchange rate, gas, and available liquidity can vary.

faq.q: What risks should I review before staking crypto?
faq.a: Review smart contract risk, validator penalties or slashing, governance changes, wallet mistakes, gas costs, tax treatment, and possible stETH price differences from ETH.

footer_note: This page is not the official app; verify stake.lido.fi before connecting a wallet; educational content only, not financial advice.
